title: Chapter Twenty: The Eternal Blossom Fist
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

The boy composed himself.

The rage bled away, replaced by focus. He gathered his energy inward, compressed it, and his stance shifted -- lower, tighter. The power of a prestigious family revealing itself. He did not waste his strength carelessly.

"I'll put my all into this," he said.

"It's the Unified Blossom Fist technique."

"You can use palm techniques too," Yeon-shin said. "Don't limit yourself. Show me everything."

The boy's jaw clenched.

Then he came.

Different this time. The footwork was lighter, more precise -- the lightest Yeon-shin had seen. The fist carried a spiral of energy around the knuckles, a drilling force meant to tear through any guard.

Yeon-shin met it with the Charged Rotational Strike, rotating in the opposite direction.

Fist met fist.

The impact rang across the marble like a bell struck with iron. Energy fragments scattered across both their sleeves. Concentration at its absolute peak.

The boy staggered back. Yeon-shin did not.

*The essence of the Charged Rotational Technique is accumulation. Build on what came before.*

He twisted his footwork. Stepped again. Energy doubled without faltering.

The second Charged Rotational Strike launched from his right hand, distorting the air around it. The blow broke through the boy's elbow defence and struck the solar plexus. Clean. Deep.

"Kuhek!"

*A fist technique that uses the Charged Rotational Technique freely. Inspired by the Unified Blossom Fist. Continuous strikes that build on each other.*

The boy corrected his stance, teeth gritted.

"I'm continuing," Yeon-shin said.

"Arrogant!"

A growl. But the boy spread his palm wide and the energy changed. His left hand redirected Yeon-shin's third strike three times -- not a feint, but a technique that built striking power through the redirection itself -- then struck with the back of his hand.

The boy stumbled backward, shaking out his own hand. The impact had been tremendous.

*Another interesting principle.*

Yeon-shin comprehended it instantly. Filed it away.

The fourth Charged Rotational Strike. The marble cracked under his foot. Strain in his muscles now, his tendons, the meridians carrying the accumulated force. Each step heavier than the last.

*Bearable.*

Too much force for the Grass Leaf Technique. Too much for subtlety. But the Radiant Demon Formation offered the Penetrating Path, and it showed where to strike.

He dropped low. The unbroken movement embodied the formation's freedom. His technique had gone beyond the Unified Blossom Fist now. A new kind of power.

His fist connected. Through the surface of his knuckles, the boy's protective energy shattered.

*Five moves was enough.*

The principle penetrating his own martial arts crystallised. Overlapping power. Accumulation without limit. How the Fate Conquering Scripture worked. How the Charged Rotational Technique worked. How he -- a boy raised in a third-rate family with five years to live -- had learned to face the world.

Sky-blue light blazed in his eyes.

Lightning surged from within.

The essence of the Unified Blossom Fist. The verses of the Fate Conquering Scripture. The accumulation principle of the Charged Rotational Technique. They converged in his mind, merging into a single key point. A fist technique that grows more powerful the longer the exchange continues. Each strike building on every strike before it.

Eternal Blossom Fist.

The name arrived with the understanding, as naturally as breathing.

He swung from beside his shoulder. Rubble whirled around his arm. The accumulated force of five advances detonated an inch from the boy's solar plexus.

The blast rolled through the boy's body. His torso rocked. His expression went blank. His arms dropped.

"A fist technique containing the essence of energy manifestation!" Ma Jin's voice from across the marble, cracking with admiration. "He's no longer bound by the rotation of the Whole Body Technique!"

"You can continue if you like," Yeon-shin said.

The boy bit his lower lip until blood welled. He said nothing.

Three figures approached from the edge.

Ma Jin looked drained. Ma Yeon-jeok's face was frozen -- not with anger, but with something far worse for a man of his pride.

Recognition.

Only the Lord of Desolate Sect was smiling.

"I witnessed a precious sight," she said. "The scene of creating a martial art. What an occasional entertainment."

"I am embarrassed. I wonder if I have only confused your eyes."

"That is an overstatement. You look very pleased." Her smile carried warmth. "Have you decided on a name?"

"I called it the Eternal Blossom Fist."

"A fist technique where time's fortune shines endlessly. A fitting name." The smile deepened. "However -- the key weakness of continuous techniques is enduring the first exchange. With your talent, you should overcome it."

"Thank you for the advice."

He clasped his fists and bowed.

On the far side of the marble, Ma Yeon-jeok crouched beside the boy. The adopted grandson was fighting tears, resentment dripping from every line of his face. The old man stroked both grandsons' heads and stared at Yeon-shin across the training ground.

"What should I do," Ma Yeon-jeok said, his voice cracking, "for you to forgive me?"

Everything stopped.

The adopted grandson's devastation. Ma Jin's shock. Even the Sect Leader's eyes widened slightly.

*He is terrifying.*

It was said that the purple masters of Desolate City acted as absolute beings every time they went out to the martial world. Like heavenly gods holding the power of life and death over most humans within reach of their energy sense. A common rumour. Unless you were at the level of the leader of the Nine Sects, revered as an immortal among civilians, you could not even cross swords with such a man.

And such a person had bowed his head. To a White-rank boy of no status.

*Because I am talented. Because he is obsessed with the revival of his family. This is why he cut ties with his daughter who married the head of the Jeong family.*

It gave him goosebumps. The kind of man who would sacrifice anything -- a daughter, a grandchild, decades of connection -- for the family's ascent. And now, seeing talent worth claiming, the madness reversed direction. Enough to make him think he should choose his words carefully.

He did not want to become *Ma* Yeon-shin.

"It is the greatest burden for you to show such an appearance, grandfather," Yeon-shin said. "The past years cannot be undone, and the people of my maternal family are no different from strangers to me. Radiant Demon Wing is my family."

"Can't we build affection?"

"I don't even have enough time to build martial arts."

It was said that even the prestige of the Desolate Ma family could not reach the fruit of the World Tree. He felt no attraction from either necessity or blood ties.

The white beard trembled.

"I won in the martial arts match your grandson recklessly requested," Yeon-shin said. "Please respect me as a martial artist."

A dumbfounded expression. Still, Yeon-shin could not feel at ease. Who knew how much honeyed talk the old man might use in the future. Presumably because he was a person with madness.

"Since Lightning Flash won, we should wrap this up," the Sect Leader said. Her melodious voice carried across the ruined marble. She looked around at the people gathered here -- Yeon-shin, Ma Yeon-jeok, the trembling adopted grandson, Ma Jin.

"I will pass on the type of martial art Lightning Flash needs when he wants. Also, Lightning Flash can make one request to the Desolate Ma family. I certify this."

"I will also convey that through the leader when I want," Yeon-shin said.

Ma Jin's expression had become deeply strange. The look of a man who had just learned he was to serve as a messenger between his own father and his sister's son. The Radiant Demon Extreme Lord of Falling Hell, reduced to a go-between.

Yeon-shin did not particularly want to fathom those feelings.

*The sin lies on their side.*

He politely greeted the Sect Leader and Ma Yeon-jeok. Then he turned.

Training was urgent. The Eternal Blossom Fist was not complete. The first five forms were rough, the accumulation unstable at higher levels. He needed to refine the transitions, smooth the buildup, find the balance between power and control. He had only laid the groundwork. He was not quite satisfied with the moves yet, and he had to rely completely on the Radiant Demon Formation when using palm techniques.

He suddenly realised he had never learned the name of the Desolate Ma family heir. He was not very curious.

Behind him, fading with distance:

"Yeon-juk. How interesting. It's a face I've never seen before. An expression of utmost regret."

"Leader. Not even helping."

"How can I help with your karma?"

He walked through the castle and out into the sunlight. Ma Jin fell into step behind him. The situation had become the opposite of when they came -- now it was the Radiant Demon Wing Leader following his junior, not leading him.

They passed a small lakeside. The sun was warm on the rippled water, casting bright patterns on the path.

"Lightning Flash."

Yeon-shin stopped.

Ma Jin's scarred reflection shimmered on the sunlit water beside his own. The Radiant Demon Wing Leader carefully opened his mouth.

"There's a mission," Ma Jin said. "One that could be helpful to you."
